title: WaterServiceRequest
description: A resident/customer request to DEP to fix a water or sewer problem — a water-main break, no water or low pressure, a leak, a SEWER BACKUP, a clogged catch basin, a hydrant problem, or discolored water — and, for account holders, water-bill and account service. NET-NEW: there is NO single public API for this. Today the transaction is split and locked: emergencies and street conditions are funneled through generic NYC311 (portal.311.nyc.gov), while billing/account service lives only inside the login-walled My DEP Account portal (a826-umax.dep.nyc.gov, the uMAX Customer Information System on ASP.NET + Azure AD B2C). DEP's own read-side ticket data surfaces separately as the 'Work Order Management Module' dataset (4fvw-nn9c). This schema serves both the read surface (list/get a customer's own requests) and the write surface (report a problem).

requestId.description: Server-assigned service-request / work-order number (maps to SVC_REQUEST_NUMBER / WORKORDER_NUMBER). Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by DEP, not the requester.

accountNumber.description: DEP water/sewer account number, for billing/account requests (validated against uMAX). Optional for street-condition reports.

emergency.description: Flag life-safety / property emergencies (water-main break, sewer backup into a home, flooding, no water). Emergencies are routed to DEP's 24-hour customer service and NYC311 priority intake.
